rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELMO LEE CARTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/2/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended petition was filed on 7/7/23 (e-filed on 7/10/23). No proof of service of the amended petition has been filed yet. [CRC 7.53(a).] (2) No courtesy copy of the amended petition has been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] (Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies.) (3) When were receipts filed? (There were six heirs and $133,697.66 in cash as of the date of death. How was Hattie Ross entitled to over $40,000?) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and REPRESENTATIVE CAPACITY, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STANLEY CHONG KWONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) James Mitchell and Kate Freeland/Daniel Bernhard, the attorneys who formerly represented the petitioner as special administrator and/or executor, have not signed a waiver of statutory compensation partially allowable in proportion to their services actually rendered to the estate. 2) The Separate Property Trust was purportedly terminated by the November 2021 settlement agreement between the petitioner and trustee of the Separate Property Trust. The petition requests final distribution of the estate cash on hand to the Joint Property Trust, even though the cash on hand represents the decedent's separate property. The decedent's will requires distribution of separate property to the Separate Property Trust. The will contains a savings provision requiring the creation of a testamentary trust on the same terms and conditions as the Separate Property Trust to receive distribution of separate property, in the event the Separate Property Trust fails for any reason presumably including termination. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARILYN A. FERRUCCI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE CONSERVATORSHIP of Person and Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/23/2023. 1) Paragraph 6 of the petition: No allegation has been made in support of the request for post-accounting service fees of the petitioner ($1,450). 2) Paragraph 7: No allegation has been made in support of the request for post-accounting service fees and costs of the petitioner's attorneys ($5,000 + $600). 3) Paragraph 13: The assets on hand include the conservatee's coins and Tupperware shares. 4) Schedules of gains and losses (pre-death accounting): Sales prices and carry values must be stated. 5) Schedule of gains (post-death accounting): i) The redemption value and gain must be separately stated for each bond. ii) If the $100 bond issued November 1984 did not name a joint owner or POD beneficiary, did the petitioner obtain court approval before redeeming the bond? See Probate Code section 2631(b). iii) Did the petitioner obtain court approval before liquidating the Blackrock shares held in the 529 plan account? 6) Schedule of property on hand as of 03/31/2023: i) The IRA and bonds naming a joint owner or POD beneficiary must be removed from the assets on hand and must be listed on a separate schedule of other credits as constructive distributions to the surviving joint owner or POD beneficiary. ii) Please provide receipts signed by the surviving joint owner and POD beneficiary or other proof of their acknowledgment of entitlement. iii) If the coins and Tupperware shares are not liquidated (with court approval per section 2631(b)) and need be transferred in kind to the devisee trust, a small estate affidavit signed by the trustee must be filed. If they have already been distributed to the trust, a receipt signed by the trustee must also be filed, and the accounting schedules must be revised to show the $0 ending balance with a distribution credit. 7) The attorney's declaration re retention of the original account statements must include language required by LRSF 14.59(A) & CRC 2.257.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition By Attorney For Deceased Co-Executor Michael Rooney For Partial Attorney?S Fees And Costs, And To Compel Executor To Sell Estate Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/2/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice was not given to the executor or his attorney. (2) There are no time sheets to support the fees requested. [CRC 7.702.] Costs must also be set forth in detail, showing the date, payee, description, and amount, as with a disbursements schedule. (3) Statutory compensation will be granted by the Court only in proportion to the work actually completed. Compensation for extraordinary services will be allowed before final distribution only when it appears likely that the estate will remain in probate for an unusually long time, whether due to litigation or other cause, or on a showing that present payment will benefit the beneficiaries or the estate. [LRSF 14.55 A.] (4) If there are two or more attorneys for the personal representative, the attorney's compensation shall be apportioned among the attorneys by the court according to the services actually rendered by each attorney or as agreed to by the attorneys. [Probate Code Section 10814.] (5) The attorney might need to file an accounting for the period the Special Administrator/Co-Executor served as sole or co-personal representative to justify their services provided while representing him. [Probate Code Section 10953(c).] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (It appears objections and a subsequent declaration were filed. Courtesy copies have not been received by the examiner as of 7/10/23. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.]) NB: A copy of the attorney's declaration filed on 7/7/23 was just received on 7/12/23. The declaration provides time sheets, but 7/7/23 was not at least 15 days before this hearing. Also, the costs listed in the declaration do not have adequate descriptions (e.g., what was filed or sent by messenger, etc.)

Whether notice can be prescribed in any other manner (telephone, e-mail, social media account, etc.) must also be addressed. The following issues remain unaddressed by Giliane Balgos: 1) Notice must be given to Karen Gardener identified as one of the predeceased spouse's issue with a known address (see declaration filed on 04/11/2023). Whether notice can be prescribed in any other manner (telephone, e-mail, social media account, etc.) must also be addressed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FREDDIE L. & MABEL I. FIELDS 2005 REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instruction To Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/27/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (Notice of Remote Appearance is not the instructions for how to appear remotely. Please see the Court's website for the exact language.) (4) A skim of the petition prayer online indicates that the trustee is seeking approval of an accounting, but the petition caption does not disclose all relief sought, and support for the accounting does not appear to have been filed. [CRC 7.102 and LRSF 14.34] The assigned examiner must receive the proposed order and courtesy copies of all filings by 9/18/23 to avoid another automatic continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
